plan.title: Build the foundation
plan.objective: Set up your personal AI-Lab and establish tool boundaries.
plan.whyThisWeek: You need a clean, personal system outside work so learning and portfolio-building do not live in chats, tmp folders, or company spend.
plan.developmentOutcome: You understand where your work lives, how tools are separated, and how your system will be extended.

tasks.title: Unlink Cursor from your work account and sign in with your personal account
tasks.why: Your learning and governance portfolio need to live in a personal environment you control.
tasks.outcome: Clear separation between company work and personal career system.

tasks.title: Create the AI-Lab folder tree on your machine
tasks.why: A file-based system gives you stable storage for workflows, notes, templates, and artifacts.
tasks.outcome: You know where every future note, template, and artifact belongs.

tasks.title: Create a top-level README that explains the purpose of each folder
tasks.why: A good system is understandable at a glance and maintainable by Cursor later.
tasks.outcome: A documented structure that can be extended without guesswork.

tasks.title: Write your 12-month target outcome in one paragraph
tasks.why: Tasks make more sense when tied to an explicit target state.
tasks.outcome: A clear north star for the whole system.

plan.title: Install the workflow pattern
plan.objective: Create the markdown-first workflow templates and run your first disciplined AI cycle.
plan.whyThisWeek: The goal is not just doing tasks. It is learning the pattern: spec -> implement -> verify -> review.
plan.developmentOutcome: You understand the difference between agentic engineering and random tool use.

tasks.title: Create spec_template.md
tasks.why: You need to define goals, constraints, risks, and done criteria before using AI seriously.
tasks.outcome: You can start any serious task with structure.

tasks.title: Create review_template.md
tasks.why: You need a repeatable way to verify output instead of trusting fluency.
tasks.outcome: You have a checklist for validation and second-pass review.

tasks.title: Create governance_memo_template.md
tasks.why: Your future pivot depends on artifacts that demonstrate governance thinking, not just code generation.
tasks.outcome: A reusable starting point for portfolio memos.

tasks.title: Run one full workflow using Cursor/Claude and Gemini on the same task
tasks.why: Comparing models teaches judgment and exposes blind spots early.
tasks.outcome: One saved example of spec -> implementation -> review.

plan.title: Extract your trainings
plan.objective: Turn your SonderMind training notes into reusable principles and templates.
plan.whyThisWeek: You already have material. This week converts passive notes into active system parts.
plan.developmentOutcome: You begin transforming existing knowledge into leverage.

tasks.title: Move your training notes into 01_learning
tasks.why: Important learning should live in your system, not a temporary folder.
tasks.outcome: Training material becomes part of your long-term knowledge base.

tasks.title: Extract 5 principles from the agentic engineering training
tasks.why: Principles guide future judgment better than raw transcripts.
tasks.outcome: A short principles file you can review weekly.

tasks.title: Create one reusable checklist from the training
tasks.why: Checklists reduce cognitive load and make good habits repeatable.
tasks.outcome: A practical tool you can use immediately.

plan.title: Model comparison discipline
plan.objective: Build a reliable process for cross-model review.
plan.whyThisWeek: You want to learn to use AI like a pro, and pros do not trust one answer path.
plan.developmentOutcome: You learn when Claude is enough, when Gemini adds value, and how to compare outputs.

tasks.title: Create a prompt_comparison_template.md
tasks.why: You need a standard way to compare outputs rather than relying on vague impressions.
tasks.outcome: A reproducible comparison process.

tasks.title: Compare Claude and Gemini on one governance question
tasks.why: This creates direct experience with strengths, weaknesses, and review habits.
tasks.outcome: A saved comparison with your conclusions.

plan.title: Governance artifact #1
plan.objective: Write your first portfolio-quality governance artifact.
plan.whyThisWeek: Your pivot will come from proof, not aspiration.
plan.developmentOutcome: You have your first credible artifact for future job conversations.

tasks.title: Write a memo on human-in-the-loop requirements for sensitive AI systems
tasks.why: This shows judgment, risk awareness, and product reasoning.
tasks.outcome: A publishable portfolio artifact.

plan.title: Workflow automation starter
plan.objective: Automate one small repeatable task on your machine.
plan.whyThisWeek: Career leverage grows when you build systems, not just documents.
plan.developmentOutcome: You prove you can connect AI to real workflow improvement.

tasks.title: Pick one tiny workflow to automate
tasks.why: Small wins build confidence without creating tool chaos.
tasks.outcome: A repeatable automation target.

tasks.title: Implement and document the automation
tasks.why: The value is not only the script. It is the repeatable pattern.
tasks.outcome: One working tool plus documentation.

plan.title: Governance artifact #2
plan.objective: Create a risk register for an AI product you understand well.
plan.whyThisWeek: Risk registers translate engineering knowledge into governance language.
plan.developmentOutcome: You practice speaking across technical and governance boundaries.

tasks.title: Create a risk register for an AI product you understand well
tasks.why: This builds directly from your domain experience.
tasks.outcome: A strong domain-relevant governance artifact.

plan.title: Job market mapping
plan.objective: Translate your developing system into target roles and hiring language.
plan.whyThisWeek: You need to know what roles your new artifacts support.
plan.developmentOutcome: You can name 10 realistic role targets and the evidence each one needs.

tasks.title: Identify 10 target AI governance / eval / oversight roles
tasks.why: A pivot needs targets, not just self-improvement.
tasks.outcome: A role map grounded in actual job types.

tasks.title: Map each role to missing skills or missing evidence
tasks.why: This turns vague ambition into measurable preparation.
tasks.outcome: A gap analysis you can act on.

plan.title: Governance artifact #3
plan.objective: Build an evaluation rubric or oversight checklist.
plan.whyThisWeek: You want artifacts that look like the work of the role you want.
plan.developmentOutcome: You strengthen your portfolio with practical governance documentation.

tasks.title: Create an AI oversight rubric for a system with meaningful human impact
tasks.why: Rubrics show your ability to operationalize judgment.
tasks.outcome: A concrete governance artifact.

plan.title: Career narrative
plan.objective: Rewrite your resume story around governance, evaluation, and oversight.
plan.whyThisWeek: A pivot succeeds when the story is legible to other people.
plan.developmentOutcome: You can explain why your background qualifies you for governance roles.

tasks.title: Write a pivot narrative: engineer -> governance/evals/oversight
tasks.why: Hiring managers need to understand the bridge.
tasks.outcome: A concise story you can reuse in applications and conversations.

tasks.title: Draft 6 governance-oriented resume bullets from your existing work
tasks.why: You need evidence phrased in the language of the new target.
tasks.outcome: A starting point for resume revision.

plan.title: Governance artifact #4
plan.objective: Create one incident response or escalation framework.
plan.whyThisWeek: Governance work is not only policy. It is operational response.
plan.developmentOutcome: You add a process-oriented artifact to your portfolio.

tasks.title: Design an escalation workflow for unsafe AI behavior
tasks.why: This demonstrates practical thinking about real-world oversight.
tasks.outcome: A process artifact useful in interviews or portfolio review.

plan.title: Quarter review and reset
plan.objective: Review the first quarter, refine the system, and set the next 3-month block.
plan.whyThisWeek: A sustainable system improves itself instead of becoming stale.
plan.developmentOutcome: You know what is actually working and what should change.

tasks.title: Review all completed work and note what created real leverage
tasks.why: You need evidence about what is actually helping your pivot.
tasks.outcome: A practical review, not just a feeling.

tasks.title: Set the next 12 weeks based on role gaps and strongest progress
tasks.why: The system should evolve from evidence, not random inspiration.
tasks.outcome: A focused next-quarter roadmap.

plan.title: Deepen automation and tooling discipline / Scope and plan
plan.objective: Scope this month precisely and choose the first concrete deliverable inside "Deepen automation and tooling discipline".
plan.whyThisWeek: The first quarter proves you can work with structure. Month 4 turns those isolated wins into a real operating environment.
plan.developmentOutcome: You have 2 to 3 documented workflows and a stable AI-Lab habit. You have a concrete scoped plan and clear definition of done for the month.

tasks.title: Select one live SonderMind initiative to use as a governance case study for workflow automation and governance controls
tasks.why: Grounding work in real initiatives increases transferability and market credibility.
tasks.outcome: A scoped case study linked to real production context and decision points.

tasks.title: Define monthly deliverable, measurable success criteria, and resume bullet target
tasks.why: Explicit criteria prevent activity drift and force outcome-driven execution.
tasks.outcome: A measurable definition of done plus draft hiring-facing language.

plan.title: Deepen automation and tooling discipline / Build
plan.objective: Do the main build work for "Deepen automation and tooling discipline".
plan.whyThisWeek: The first quarter proves you can work with structure. Month 4 turns those isolated wins into a real operating environment.
plan.developmentOutcome: You have 2 to 3 documented workflows and a stable AI-Lab habit. You have real, visible work in progress instead of only planning.

tasks.title: Build the core artifact for workflow automation and governance controls using real eval/chat/governance data
tasks.why: Real data and real constraints produce artifacts that stand up in interviews.
tasks.outcome: A substantive artifact draft with evidence and design rationale.

tasks.title: Capture before/after metrics and implementation notes from the workflow
tasks.why: Quantified impact is required for resume strength and hiring conversion.
tasks.outcome: A metrics-backed evidence pack for portfolio and resume use.

plan.title: Deepen automation and tooling discipline / Refine
plan.objective: Review, refine, and improve the quality of work produced this month.
plan.whyThisWeek: The first quarter proves you can work with structure. Month 4 turns those isolated wins into a real operating environment.
plan.developmentOutcome: You have 2 to 3 documented workflows and a stable AI-Lab habit. You have stronger, more polished output with fewer weak spots.

tasks.title: Run adversarial review (Gemini + checklist + stakeholder lens) on the artifact
tasks.why: Cross-model and stakeholder challenge reduces blind spots and weak assumptions.
tasks.outcome: A reviewed artifact with explicit risk findings and remediation actions.

tasks.title: Revise artifact and log governance decisions, tradeoffs, and remaining risks
tasks.why: Decision logs demonstrate governance maturity beyond surface-level output.
tasks.outcome: A stronger artifact plus decision trail suitable for interviews.

plan.title: Deepen automation and tooling discipline / Package and review
plan.objective: Package the month's work, summarize lessons learned, and prepare the next cycle.
plan.whyThisWeek: The first quarter proves you can work with structure. Month 4 turns those isolated wins into a real operating environment.
plan.developmentOutcome: You have 2 to 3 documented workflows and a stable AI-Lab habit. You have a finished monthly package and a clean transition into the next cycle.

tasks.why: Career transitions require translation of work into market language and forward planning.

notes.1: In 12 months, I want to be someone who can do real AI governance work. I want to use what I already do well—AI evals, user-facing chat work, and cross-team engineering—to build clear proof that I can handle AI risk, quality, and accountability. I want a working personal system that helps me ship one meaningful artifact at a time, save what works, and keep improving without burning out. I want to be ready for responsible AI / AI governance roles that are stable, meaningful, and pay at least what I make now in Colorado. Most of all, I want this to be a practical transition from overwork and stress into a sustainable career with real impact.

context.whyThisExists: This project is a personal AI governance operating system that turns learning into visible career proof and resume-ready evidence. It exists to make weekly work repeatable, measurable, and portfolio-generating instead of scattered across chats and temporary notes.
context.primaryGoal: Build a 12-month governance workbench that leverages current work strengths, produces resume artifacts, supports a lower-stress trajectory, and transitions into resilient AI governance roles.
operatingSystem: Production lane: directly leverage current strengths in evals, user-facing chat apps, and governance committee participation.
Learning lane: train continuously on prompting, orchestration, model evaluation, and automation.
Career-proof lane: ship one governance artifact each month for interview and resume-quality evidence.
Store reusable prompts, templates, checklists, and outputs in the AI-Lab file system.
toolStrategy: Use personal Claude for planning, governance writing, and personal experimentation.
Use Claude Code for repo-aware implementation and terminal workflows when context matters.
Use Gemini as second-pass review to challenge assumptions and expose blind spots.
Keep tooling lightweight and file-based first; avoid buying an overcomplicated productivity stack.
weeklyRhythm: Monday: refine one real workflow from production practice.
Tuesday: learn one new orchestration/tooling concept.
Wednesday: compare outputs across 2-3 models on the same task.
Thursday: build or revise one governance artifact.
Friday: write a short portfolio note on learning, automation wins, and governance insights.
guardrails: Agentic engineering over vibe coding: plan -> spec -> implement -> verify -> review.
Do not trust a single polished answer; compare systems and verify.
Keep personal AI development separate from company spend and repos.
AI is a tool, not authority; accountability, guardrails, and human oversight are required.
nearTermOutcomes: Become AI-native with disciplined prompt/system instruction habits while leveraging real job projects.
Build reusable workflow templates and automation helpers.
Ship governance artifacts: risk register, eval rubric, incident workflow, policy memo, and resume-ready outcome summaries.
Grow role-ready evidence for governance/evals/oversight opportunities with a sustainable workload profile.

notes.1: Wildcore placeholder note. Replace with your active plan.

context.whyThisExists: This project is Akira's four-pillar AI curriculum with an animal mission. It exists to build real AI skill, builder thinking, truth-verification habits, and healthy human-centered AI use.
context.primaryGoal: Develop Akira's Wild AI Ranger Academy: Use AI. Help Animals. Stay Human.
operatingSystem: Pillar 1 - AI Explorer: ask better questions, refine prompts, research, and create.
Pillar 2 - Builder Brain: learn coding logic, data basics, and how AI systems are built.
Pillar 3 - Truth Detective: compare answers, verify sources, and avoid gullibility.
Pillar 4 - Human Heart: keep real people, real animals, and real life above AI interaction.
toolStrategy: Use multiple AI tools for comparison and critical thinking, not single-answer trust.
Anchor projects in animal care, habitat ideas, and creative nature-based tasks.
Introduce simple automation and project-building progressively.
Keep sessions practical, playful, and grounded in real-world curiosity.
weeklyRhythm: One exploration session: better questions and prompt refinement.
One building session: make/code something small.
One truth session: compare outputs and verify what is real.
One human session: connect learning to animals, family, and offline life.
guardrails: Smooth confidence is not truth; always ask how we know.
AI is a tool, not a best friend.
Verify important claims with trusted or official sources.
Protect attention, compassion, and real relationships.
nearTermOutcomes: Run a consistent four-pillar weekly routine.
Create first animal-centered mini projects and reflections.
Build strong early habits for skepticism and verification.
Keep AI learning fun, ethical, and deeply human.

plan.title: Build the foundation
plan.objective: Set up your personal AI-Lab and establish tool boundaries.
plan.whyThisWeek: You need a clean, personal system outside work so learning and portfolio-building do not live in chats, tmp folders, or company spend.
plan.developmentOutcome: You understand where your work lives, how tools are separated, and how your system will be extended.

tasks.title: Unlink Cursor from your work account and sign in with your personal account
tasks.why: Your learning and governance portfolio need to live in a personal environment you control.
tasks.outcome: Clear separation between company work and personal career system.

tasks.title: Create the AI-Lab folder tree on your machine
tasks.why: A file-based system gives you stable storage for workflows, notes, templates, and artifacts.
tasks.outcome: You know where every future note, template, and artifact belongs.

tasks.title: Create a top-level README that explains the purpose of each folder
tasks.why: A good system is understandable at a glance and maintainable by Cursor later.
tasks.outcome: A documented structure that can be extended without guesswork.

tasks.title: Write your 12-month target outcome in one paragraph
tasks.why: Tasks make more sense when tied to an explicit target state.
tasks.outcome: A clear north star for the whole system.

tasks.why: Initial placeholder seed

plan.objective: Create the markdown-first workflow templates and run your first disciplined AI cycle.
plan.whyThisWeek: The goal is not just doing tasks. It is learning the pattern: spec -> implement -> verify -> review.
plan.developmentOutcome: You understand the difference between agentic engineering and random tool use.

tasks.why: You need to define goals, constraints, risks, and done criteria before using AI seriously.
tasks.outcome: You can start any serious task with structure.

tasks.title: Create review_template.md

notes.1: Holly Case Management placeholder note. Replace with your active plan.

context.whyThisExists: This project is Holly's education-plus-AI operating system for case management. It exists to build practical, ethical AI fluency that supports human care without replacing the human relationship.
context.primaryGoal: Build a sustainable path into case management with AI-assisted workflows and strong verification discipline.
operatingSystem: Education lane: choose and validate one target case-management path with realistic entry steps.
Workflow lane: build repeatable systems for intake notes, summaries, emails, and resource plans.
Verification lane: never use unverified program details or policy guidance from one AI answer.
toolStrategy: Use AI to draft intake notes from bullets and turn meetings into action lists.
Use AI for resource discovery and summary drafts before manual verification.
Use AI to produce clearer client/family communications and checklists.
Cross-check with another model/source, then verify with official organization/policy pages.
weeklyRhythm: Complete one lane-specific objective and one concrete deliverable per week.
Run one note-to-summary workflow and one research-and-verify workflow each week.
Add or refine templates for emails, case summaries, and resource plans.
End each week with reflection on what improved support quality and reduced admin burden.
guardrails: AI is a co-pilot for admin and planning, not a replacement for human trust and judgment.
Never trust first-answer fluency on programs, benefits, or policy details.
Keep communication clear, ethical, and client-centered.
nearTermOutcomes: Confirm target case-management lane and local entry requirements.
Build starter workflow templates for summaries, follow-ups, and resource comparisons.
Produce one sample portfolio packet showing ethical AI-assisted case support.
Demonstrate reliable verification habits on every high-impact detail.
